definition: The core PCE 'stall zone' of 2.5-3.5% has a historical duration of 18-36 months from goods disinflation completion, with a fat right tail to 48+ months. Markets pricing 12-month resolution are in the left tail. Equity markets rally during the stall zone in 4/5 analogues (median +18%), ending 3-6 months before resolution.
evidence: 5 episodes: median 24 months, mean 25 months, range 15-36+
4/5 analogues show equity rally during stall zone (median +18%), ending 3-6 months before resolution
open_questions: Could structural novelties compress or extend the stall zone duration?
What signals reliably indicate the stall zone is ending?
Is the equity rally pattern reliable enough to be actionable?
